insight:
Yesterday was a solid active recovery day with 9,653 steps and no formal training, which is smart positioning just 6 days out from Chester. Your 2,471 calories burned shows good metabolic activity even on a rest day. However, that 5.8 hours of sleep is concerning this close to race day - you need quality recovery time right now.

Your HRV at 95ms sits perfectly within your baseline range of 93-115ms, and that 49 bpm resting heart rate shows excellent cardiovascular fitness. With stress levels low at 34/100, your body is responding well to the taper. These recovery markers give you the green light to proceed with today's planned session without modification.

Focus on sleep hygiene tonight - aim for 7-8 hours to support your final week of preparation. Your body is primed and ready, but adequate sleep will be crucial for peak performance on race day.
